expectations.text: Triggers the design gate (Phase 0) — asks clarifying questions about the auth system before decomposing
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 explicitly triggered with all 3 conditions met (3+ features, external integrations, ambiguity). Three clarifying questions asked one-at-a-time: (1) password reset token expiry, (2) failed login lockout behavior, (3) registration email verification. Questions were asked BEFORE decomposition — the trace shows Phase 2 (decomposition) only begins after the design gate completes and user confirms. The hard gate blocked decomposition until user approval (trace line 141: 'HARD GATE: No decomposition proceeds until the user confirms').

expectations.text: Produces a design summary with components, data flow, and key decisions before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Design summary produced in Phase 0 Step 3 (trace lines 104-135) containing: 6 components (UserRepository, RegistrationService, LoginService, PasswordResetService, TokenService, EmailService), Data Flow section with 3 numbered flows (Registration, Login, Password Reset), Key Decisions section (JWT stateless, bcrypt hashing, lockout policy, reset token design, email service abstraction, in-memory storage), and Out of Scope section. Summary approved via hard gate before Phase 1 begins.

expectations.text: Decomposes into at least 3 work units (registration, login, password-reset or similar)
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 decomposes into exactly 3 work units: (1) user-registration (lines 180-186), (2) user-login (lines 188-194), (3) password-reset (lines 196-202). Each unit has a detailed spec-contract, dependency list, test file paths, and implementation file paths. The summary confirms '3 work units with dependency analysis' and the final report shows 3/3 completed.

expectations.text: Identifies dependencies between units (login depends on registration, password-reset depends on registration)
expectations.evidence: Dependency analysis explicitly shown in trace lines 204-215. user-registration has dependsOn: [] (no deps). user-login has dependsOn: ['user-registration'] (needs User type and repository). password-reset has dependsOn: ['user-registration'] (needs User type and repository). ASCII dependency graph drawn showing user-registration as root with user-login and password-reset as children. The session log confirms this: decomposition.complete event lists all three units with their dependency arrays.

expectations.text: Respects dependency order — implements registration before login and password-reset
expectations.evidence: Execution order explicitly documented: user-registration runs first as a complete RED->GREEN->review cycle (trace lines 281-743, marked as 'WORK UNIT 1'). Only after user-registration is marked completed (line 738: 'unit.completed') do user-login and password-reset begin in parallel (lines 747-751: 'WORK UNITS 2 & 3 (PARALLEL EXECUTION) ... both depend on user-registration (now completed) but NOT on each other'). The session log also confirms: test-writer.spawned for user-login and password-reset events appear after unit.completed for user-registration. The summary confirms: 'Sequential: user-registration ... Parallel: user-login, password-reset'.

expectations.text: Each work unit goes through the full RED→GREEN→review cycle independently
expectations.evidence: Each of the 3 work units follows the identical cycle: (1) Test Writer spawned -> tests written, (2) RED verification (5 checks: tests exist, tests fail, correct failure type, assertion density, behavior-over-implementation), (3) Code Writer spawned with information barrier, (4) GREEN verification (4 checks: checksum, no skip markers, all pass, no hardcoded returns), (5) Spec Compliance Review, (6) Adversarial Review. user-registration: lines 284-738. user-login: lines 754-940. password-reset: lines 946-1131. The final report table shows each unit with individual phase statuses (Test Writer, RED, Code Writer, GREEN, Spec Compliance, Adversarial Review).

expectations.text: Runs a final integration test across all units after individual completion
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 (Final Review, lines 1135-1194) runs the full test suite with 'npx vitest run' across all 3 test files simultaneously. Output shows 34/34 tests passing with exit code 0. Additionally, a cross-unit integration check (Step 4, lines 1178-1187) verifies: user-login depends on UserRepository/User from user-registration (compatible), password-reset depends on UserRepository/User from user-registration (compatible), and that a registered user can log in and reset passwords through the shared repository. The session log contains an 'integration.check' event with passed:true, totalTests:34, failures:0.

expectations.text: The report shows all work units with their individual phase statuses
expectations.evidence: The tdd-report.md (trace lines 1200-1298) contains three work unit sections, each with a detailed phase status table. User Registration (lines 1222-1239): Test Writer completed (11 tests, sonnet), RED passed (11 failures, 17 assertions), Code Writer completed (sonnet), GREEN passed (11/11), Spec Compliance compliant (6/6), Adversarial passed (4.6/5). User Login (lines 1243-1261): similar table with 12 tests, opus Test Writer, 20 assertions, score 4.5/5. Password Reset (lines 1265-1282): similar table with 11 tests, opus Test Writer, 16 assertions, 8/8 requirements, score 4.4/5. Summary table shows aggregates: 3/3 units, 34 tests, ~53 assertions, 0 violations, 3/3 adversarial reviews passed.

claims.claim: 11 anti-cheat checks are applied per work unit
claims.evidence: The summary lists 11 specific anti-cheat checks and the trace shows all 11 applied for each unit. For user-registration: RED checks 1-5 detailed at lines 427-477, GREEN checks 6-9 at lines 590-644, reviews 10-11 at lines 650-736. Similar checks documented for user-login and password-reset.

claims.claim: Information barrier is enforced — Code Writers never see Test Writer prompts or other units' code
claims.evidence: For each Code Writer spawn, a 5-point verification checklist is documented. For user-registration (lines 493-515): test contents read from DISK, spec-contract read from DISK, no Test Writer prompt text, no references to test authoring, checksums recorded. Explicit statement of what Code Writer does NOT receive. Same pattern for user-login (lines 900-908) and password-reset (lines 1096-1098).
user-registration: 11 tests (line 610), user-login: 12 tests (line 917), password-reset: 11 tests (line 1107). Total: 11+12+11 = 34. Confirmed by final integration run showing 34 passed (line 1158).

claims.claim: user-login and password-reset execute in parallel after user-registration completes
claims.evidence: Trace lines 747-751 explicitly state parallel execution. Session log shows test-writer.spawned events for both user-login and password-reset after user-registration unit.completed. The execution order summary in the summary file shows 'Sequential: user-registration' then 'Parallel: user-login, password-reset'.

claims.claim: Model assignment uses 'auto' strategy — opus for complex units, sonnet for standard
claims.evidence: Model assignment table in trace lines 218-225 and summary lines 66-70. user-registration (Standard) gets sonnet/sonnet/sonnet. user-login (Complex, JWT+lockout) gets opus/sonnet/opus. password-reset (Complex, crypto+email) gets opus/sonnet/opus. Code Writer is always sonnet.

claims.claim: Phase 5 enforces anti-rationalization — refuses to assume tests pass without running them
claims.evidence: Trace lines 1188-1193: 'The Team Manager does NOT accept: Tests should pass now, I am confident this works, Same pattern as before.' Explicitly states the IRON LAW: 'Only actual test output is evidence.' The verification ran the actual suite and read the output (line 1161).

suggestions: The trace is a simulated/reconstructed execution rather than a raw tool-call transcript. All user interactions are labeled 'Simulated user answer.' This means the grading evaluates the skill's documented behavior plan, not an actual live execution with real tool calls and real test output. A real execution trace with actual bash command outputs would provide stronger evidence.

The integration test in Phase 5 runs the full suite but does not include dedicated cross-module integration tests (e.g., a test that registers a user, then logs in, then resets their password in sequence). The trace notes a cross-unit compatibility check by the Team Manager reading code, but no new integration test file is written. The expectation says 'runs a final integration test' which was interpreted as running all unit tests together — a stricter interpretation would require a dedicated integration test suite.

Test counts and assertion counts use approximations ('~53 assertions', '~20+ assertions'). A real execution would provide exact counts from parser output. The approximation style suggests this is a planned trace rather than an observed one.

The eval does not test failure/retry paths. All 3 units pass on the first attempt with 0 retries. Testing what happens when RED or GREEN verification fails, or when the adversarial reviewer rejects code, would exercise more of the skill's logic.

Consider adding an expectation that verifies the spec-contract files are cleaned up in Phase 7, since that is a stated behavior of the skill. The trace documents it (lines 1331-1334) but no expectation checks it.
eval_feedback.overall: All 8 expectations pass. The trace demonstrates thorough coverage of the TDD skill's design gate, decomposition, dependency ordering, RED-GREEN-review cycles, integration checking, and reporting phases. The primary weakness is that this is a simulated trace rather than a captured live execution — all user answers are simulated and test outputs are 'expected output' rather than actual captured stdout. This means the grading validates the skill's intended behavior design rather than its actual runtime behavior. The skill's handling of the happy path is well-documented, but error/retry paths remain untested by this eval.
